page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8a.xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 9T24 processor running at 2.7 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM. This configuration yields a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance operates on the x86_64 architecture and does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core CPU capabilities, with stress-ng single-core performance ranking in the top 25%. However, multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores sit in the average tier. The system supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, vector neural network instructions (AVX512_VNNI), and cryptography acceleration via AES and SHA_NI. Memory write bandwidth for large blocks is average, whereas read-only memory bandwidth and database operations fall into the lower 25% performance bracket.

Given its high memory density relative to compute resources, the ecs.r8a.xlarge offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require high-speed local storage or intensive multi-threaded processing. It is well-suited for in-memory caching, Redis deployments, and lightweight database applications that fit entirely within its 32.0 GB memory footprint.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r8a.xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 9T24 processor clocked at 2.7 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing a high 8.0 GB of memory per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data highlights strong single-core CPU performance, while multi-core execution and memory write bandwidth remain average. Conversely, read-only memory bandwidth and database operations perform in the lower tier. This hardware profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads that do not demand local storage. It is suitable for in-memory caching, Redis key-value stores, and moderate database operations.
